diagrams.title: Multicore Processor Architecture
diagrams.description: A top-down view of a typical multicore processor showing multiple CPU cores, their individual caches, shared mid-level caches, memory controller, and on-chip interconnect. This helps visualize how modern CPUs achieve parallelism through multiple independent execution units.
elements: CPU Core 1 (with L1 Instruction Cache, L1 Data Cache)
CPU Core 2 (with L1 Instruction Cache, L1 Data Cache)
CPU Core 3 (with L1 Instruction Cache, L1 Data Cache)
CPU Core 4 (with L1 Instruction Cache, L1 Data Cache)
diagrams.purpose: Explains the internal structure of a multicore CPU, showing the hierarchy of caches and how cores communicate with each other and main memory

diagrams.title: GPU Architecture - Streaming Multiprocessor
diagrams.description: A detailed view of GPU architecture showing the array of Streaming Multiprocessors (SMs), each containing hundreds of small cores (ALUs/FPUs), shared memory, L1 cache, and the global memory hierarchy. This illustrates the massive parallel processing capability of GPUs.
elements: Within SM: Multiple CUDA Cores / ALUs
Within SM: Shared Memory / L1 Cache
diagrams.purpose: Explains the massively parallel architecture of GPUs, showing how thousands of simple cores are organized to handle SIMT (Single Instruction Multiple Thread) workloads

diagrams.title: CPU-GPU Heterogeneous Computing System
diagrams.description: A system-level diagram showing how the CPU (multicore processor) and GPU work together in a heterogeneous computing system. Shows the separate memory spaces, PCIe interconnect, and how tasks are offloaded from CPU to GPU for parallel execution.
elements: Data Transfer Arrows (CPU to GPU, GPU to CPU)
diagrams.purpose: Explains heterogeneous computing and how CPU and GPU collaborate, showing memory transfers and compute offloading patterns used in GPU computing frameworks like CUDA
